# Phase 1: Critical Workflow - Testing & Launch Checklist

**Version:** 1.0  
**Last Updated:** November 2, 2025  
**Purpose:** Get core appraisal workflow functioning and client-ready

---

## Overview

Phase 1 is the **minimum viable workflow** that must work perfectly before accepting real client jobs. This is not about features - it's about the essential path working end-to-end with professional client experience.

**The Core Path:**
```
Client Form → Dashboard → Valcre → LOE Email → Signature → Payment → Ready to Work
```

Everything else (N8N automations, Houski, advanced features) is Phase 2+.

---

## The Workflow (Visual)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ CLIENT SIDE                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

1. Client fills form on valta.ca
         │
         ▼
2. Receives confirmation email
         │
         ▼
[WAIT - Internal processing]
         │
         ▼
3. Receives LOE email (DocuSeal link)
         │
         ▼
4. Opens signing page (looks like Valta site)
         │
         ▼
5. Signs LOE
         │
         ▼
6. Receives payment follow-up email
         │
         ▼
7. Opens payment page (looks like Valta site)
         │
         ▼
8. Completes payment
         │
         ▼
9. Receives confirmation & next steps

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ INTERNAL SIDE (Chris & Team)                                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

1. Form submission hits Supabase
         │
         ├──────────────────────┐
         │                      │
         ▼                      ▼
2. Dashboard updated     ClickUp task created
   (Section 1 filled)    "PENDING - Property"
         │
         ▼
3. Chris reviews & fills Section 2
   (Fee, retainer, delivery date)
         │
         ▼
4. Creates Valcre job (returns VAL#####)
         │
         ├──────────────────────┐
         │                      │
         ▼                      ▼
5. VAL# saved        ClickUp task updated
   in database       "VAL##### - Property"
         │
         ▼
6. Generates LOE (preview modal)
         │
         ▼
7. Sends LOE email to client
         │
         ▼
8. Client signs → Webhook fires
         │
         ├──────────────────────┐
         │                      │
         ▼                      ▼
9. Status: "loe_signed"  ClickUp: "LOE Signed"
         │
         ▼
10. Payment follow-up triggered
         │
         ▼
11. Payment received → Job ready
```

---

## Functional Testing Checklist

### 1. Form Submission & Database

- [ ] Client form accessible from valta.ca
- [ ] All required fields validate properly
- [ ] Optional fields accept empty values
- [ ] Form submits successfully
- [ ] Data appears in Supabase `job_submissions` table
- [ ] All fields map correctly to database columns
- [ ] Job number auto-generated (format: APR-######)
- [ ] Confirmation email sent to client (if implemented)

**Test with:** At least 3 different property types to verify field handling

---

### 2. ClickUp Auto-Creation

- [ ] Task created in ClickUp immediately on form submission
- [ ] Task name: "PENDING - [Property Name], [City]"
- [ ] Task created in correct list/workspace
- [ ] 9 subtasks auto-added from template
- [ ] `clickup_task_id` saved to database
- [ ] `clickup_task_url` saved to database
- [ ] Dashboard button shows "View in ClickUp" (not "Create")
- [ ] Button link opens correct ClickUp task

**Test trigger:** Submit test form, check ClickUp within 30 seconds

**Known Issue:** If task doesn't auto-create, check webhook configuration in Supabase

---

### 3. Dashboard Display

- [ ] Job appears in dashboard list
- [ ] Job details page accessible via URL: `/dashboard/job/[id]`
- [ ] Section 1 displays all client/property info correctly
- [ ] Section 1 is read-only (no editing)
- [ ] Section 2 fields are editable
- [ ] Section 3 fields are editable
- [ ] Auto-save works on blur (field loses focus)
- [ ] "Saved" notification appears after auto-save
- [ ] No data loss when navigating away and returning

---

### 4. Valcre Job Creation

- [ ] "Create Valcre Job" button visible in Section 2
- [ ] Button only clickable when required fields filled
- [ ] Click triggers Valcre API call
- [ ] Success returns VAL##### number
- [ ] VAL# saved to database (`job_loe_details.valcre_job_id`)
- [ ] Button changes to "View Valcre Job"
- [ ] Button link opens correct Valcre job in new tab

**Field Mapping Verification:**
- [ ] Client name (First + Last) → Contact entity
- [ ] Property address → Property entity
- [ ] Appraisal fee ($ stripped) → Fee field
- [ ] Retainer ($ stripped) → Retainer field (NOT RetainerAmount)
- [ ] Property type matches Valcre dropdown exactly
- [ ] All Section 2 fields map correctly

**Test with:** Real Valcre credentials, check job appears in Valcre system

---

### 5. ClickUp Task Update (After Valcre)

- [ ] After Valcre job created, ClickUp task name updates
- [ ] New name: "VAL##### - [Property Name], [City]"
- [ ] Update happens automatically (no manual action)
- [ ] Task description includes dashboard link
- [ ] Task description includes Valcre job link
- [ ] Update appears in ClickUp within 1 minute

**If this doesn't work:** Check trigger in Valcre creation code

---

### 6. LOE Generation

- [ ] "Preview & Send LOE" button visible in Section 2
- [ ] Button only clickable after Valcre job created
- [ ] Click opens modal with LOE preview
- [ ] LOE displays all job details correctly
- [ ] 4-page format displays properly
- [ ] Professional formatting maintained
- [ ] Email recipient field pre-filled (client email)
- [ ] Can override email for testing

---

### 7. Email Delivery

- [ ] "Send to Client" button in LOE modal
- [ ] Click triggers email via Resend API
- [ ] Email arrives in client inbox (check spam folder too)
- [ ] FROM shows: Valta Appraisals <admin@valta.ca>
- [ ] Subject clear and professional
- [ ] Email body formatted properly
- [ ] DocuSeal signing link included
- [ ] Link is clickable

**Test with:** Multiple email providers (Gmail, Outlook, etc.)

**Timing:** Email should arrive within 1-2 minutes

---

### 8. DocuSeal Signing Page

- [ ] Signing link opens DocuSeal page
- [ ] Page loads without errors
- [ ] LOE document displays correctly
- [ ] Signature fields visible and functional
- [ ] Date fields auto-populate
- [ ] Can complete signature (draw, type, or upload)
- [ ] "Submit" button works
- [ ] Submission triggers webhook

**CLIENT EXPERIENCE (CRITICAL):**
- [ ] **Page styled to match valta.ca branding**
- [ ] **NO DocuSeal branding visible**
- [ ] **Same colors as Valta website**
- [ ] **Same fonts as Valta website**
- [ ] **Logo placement matches Valta site**
- [ ] **Client feels like they're still "on Valta"**
- [ ] **No jarring "where am I?" experience**

**Test with:** Someone unfamiliar with the system - do they notice they left Valta's site?

---

### 9. Signature Webhook & Status Update

- [ ] After signature submitted, webhook fires
- [ ] Job status updates to "loe_signed" in database
- [ ] Update appears in dashboard
- [ ] `loe_signed_at` timestamp recorded
- [ ] Signed document stored/accessible
- [ ] ClickUp task updated: Status or comment shows "LOE Signed"

**Timing:** Should happen within 30 seconds of signature

**If webhook doesn't fire:** Check DocuSeal webhook configuration

---

### 10. Payment Follow-Up Email (N8N Automation)

**N8N Workflow triggers on LOE signature:**
- [ ] DocuSeal webhook fires to N8N
- [ ] N8N queries Supabase for job details (amount, client email)
- [ ] N8N creates Stripe checkout session
- [ ] N8N generates payment email with checkout link
- [ ] Email sent via Resend (FROM: admin@valta.ca)
- [ ] Email arrives in client inbox
- [ ] Email content professional (Valta branding)

- [ ] ClickUp task commented: "Payment email sent - $X,XXX"

### 11. Payment Page (Stripe Checkout / Next.js)

**Approach:** Stripe Checkout embedded in Next.js page on APR Dashboard

- [ ] Payment link opens checkout page on dashboard domain
- [ ] Page loads without errors
- [ ] Stripe payment form displays correctly
- [ ] All payment fields functional (card, billing info)
- [ ] Can complete test payment in Stripe test mode

**CLIENT EXPERIENCE (CRITICAL):**
- [ ] **Page styled to match valta.ca branding**
- [ ] **Same colors, fonts, logo as Valta site**
- [ ] **Seamless visual continuation from LOE email**
- [ ] **Looks like client never left Valta ecosystem**
- [ ] **Minimal Stripe branding (powered by Stripe badge acceptable)**
- [ ] **Professional, trustworthy appearance**
- [ ] **Responsive design (works on mobile)**

**Implementation:**
- Next.js page at `/payment/:jobId` or `/checkout/:sessionId`
- Stripe Checkout embedded (iframe or redirect)
- Valta branding wrapper around Stripe form
- SSL certificate on dashboard domain

**Test on:**
- [ ] Desktop Chrome
- [ ] Desktop Safari
- [ ] Mobile iOS
- [ ] Mobile Android

**Test with:** Someone who doesn't know the system - do they trust entering payment info?

---

### 12. Payment Confirmation & Job Activation (N8N Automation)

**N8N Workflow triggers on Stripe payment success:**
- [ ] Stripe webhook fires to N8N
- [ ] N8N updates Supabase: `payment_received = true`, `payment_date = now()`
- [ ] Dashboard reflects updated status
- [ ] N8N sends confirmation email to client (receipt + next steps)

- [ ] ClickUp task updated: "Payment received $X,XXX - Job ready to work"

## Success Criteria

**Phase 1 is COMPLETE when:**

✅ All functional tests pass  
✅ Client experience is seamless (visual continuity verified)  
✅ Payment setup complete (banking info gathered, processor configured)  
✅ Chris and team confident in workflow  
✅ Ready to accept real client jobs  

**Phase 1 is NOT complete if:**

❌ Any step in core workflow broken  
❌ DocuSeal or payment page looks generic/unbranded  
❌ ClickUp tasks not auto-creating  
❌ Valcre field mapping errors  
❌ Team has concerns or confusion

# Systems Guide Update: Replace GHL with N8N Payment Automation

**Date:** November 2, 2025  
**Purpose:** Update Systems Guide v3.1 to replace GHL automation with N8N payment workflows

---

## Context

**Decision Made:** Replace GHL (Go High Level) automation with N8N workflows for Phase 1 payment automation.

**Reasons:**
- Client works in Pipedrive (not GHL)
- N8N already integrated and needed for Phase 2
- Simpler stack (fewer platforms to manage)
- More flexible and customizable
- Gmail + N8N + Pipedrive already working together

---

## Changes Required in Systems Guide v3.1

### 1. Remove/Replace GHL Section (Lines 862-900)

**Current:** GHL Automation section with white-labeled platform, email sequences, payment landing pages

**Replace with:** N8N Payment Automation section

**New content:**
```markdown
### N8N Payment Automation

**Purpose:** Automated payment workflow from LOE signature to payment confirmation

**Workflows:**

1. **Payment Email Workflow**
   - Trigger: DocuSeal webhook (LOE signed)
   - Query: Supabase (job details, amount, client email)
   - Create: Stripe checkout session
   - Generate: Payment email with checkout link
   - Send: Via Resend (admin@valta.ca)
   - Update: Pipedrive deal ("Payment Requested")
   - Update: ClickUp task ("Payment email sent - $X,XXX")

2. **Payment Confirmation Workflow**
   - Trigger: Stripe webhook (payment successful)
   - Update: Supabase (`payment_received = true`, `payment_date = now()`)
   - Send: Confirmation email to client (receipt + next steps)
   - Update: Pipedrive deal ("Payment Received - Job Active")
   - Update: ClickUp task ("Payment received $X,XXX - Job ready")
   - Status: Job ready to work

**Integration Points:**
- Supabase (job data)
- Stripe API (checkout sessions, webhooks)
- Resend (email delivery)
- Pipedrive (deal updates)
- ClickUp (task updates)

**Configuration:**
- N8N Cloud or self-hosted
- Workflows stored in N8N instance
- Webhooks configured in Stripe and DocuSeal
```

---

### 2. Update Payment Page Section

**Current:** References to GHL landing page, white-labeling

**Replace with:** Stripe Checkout embedded in Next.js

**New content:**
```markdown
### Payment Page Implementation

**Approach:** Stripe Checkout embedded in Next.js page on APR Dashboard

**Technical Details:**
- Next.js page: `/payment/:jobId` or `/checkout/:sessionId`
- Stripe Checkout: Embedded (iframe) or redirect mode
- Branding: Valta colors, fonts, logo wrapped around Stripe form
- Domain: Hosted on dashboard domain (SSL certificate)
- Responsive: Works on desktop and mobile

**Branding Requirements:**
- Page styled to match valta.ca
- Seamless continuation from LOE email
- Minimal Stripe branding ("Powered by Stripe" badge acceptable)
- Professional, trustworthy appearance

**Payment Flow:**
1. Client receives payment email (N8N workflow)
2. Clicks payment link
3. Loads Next.js page with Valta branding
4. Stripe Checkout form embedded
5. Client completes payment
6. Stripe webhook fires to N8N
7. N8N updates all systems
```

---

### 3. Update Technical Configurations Section

**Add Stripe Configuration:**

```markdown
### Stripe Configuration

**Purpose:** Payment processing for client invoices

**API Credentials:**
```javascript
Publishable Key: [pk_live_...]  // Safe for frontend
Secret Key: [sk_live_...]       // Server-side only
Webhook Secret: [whsec_...]     // For webhook signature verification
```

**Webhook Endpoints:**
- Payment Success: `https://[n8n-instance]/webhook/stripe-payment-success`
- Payment Failed: `https://[n8n-instance]/webhook/stripe-payment-failed`

**Test Mode:**
- Use separate test API keys for development
- Test card: 4242 4242 4242 4242 (any future expiry, any CVC)

**Integration:**
- Checkout sessions created via Stripe API (in N8N workflow)
- Webhooks trigger N8N automation
- Payment confirmation updates Supabase, Pipedrive, ClickUp
```

### N8N Configuration

**Purpose:** Payment automation workflows

- Stripe Secret key
- Resend API key
- Pipedrive API key
- ClickUp API key

**Webhook URLs:**
- From DocuSeal: `https://[n8n-instance]/webhook/loe-signed`
- From Stripe: `https://[n8n-instance]/webhook/stripe-payment`
```

## 🎯 What This Achieves

**Technical Simplification:**
- ❌ Removed: GHL (another platform, another login, white-labeling complexity)
- ✅ Added: N8N (already have it, need it for Phase 2 anyway)
- ✅ Added: Stripe (industry standard, easy integration)
- ✅ Added: Next.js payment page (matches dashboard, full control)

**Client Workflow:**
- ✅ Client stays in Pipedrive (where they want to be)
- ✅ Client stays in Outlook (their email)
- ✅ No pressure to move to new CRM
- ✅ Simpler tech stack = easier support

**Payment Flow (New):**
```
LOE Signed (DocuSeal)
     ↓
N8N Workflow Triggered
     ↓
Creates Stripe Checkout Session
     ↓
Sends Email (Resend: admin@valta.ca)
     ↓
Client clicks payment link
     ↓
Next.js page (Valta branded) + Stripe Checkout
     ↓
Payment processed
     ↓
Stripe Webhook → N8N
     ↓
N8N updates: Supabase, Pipedrive, ClickUp
     ↓
Confirmation email sent
```

**No GHL anywhere in the flow.**

Summary:
## 1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Initial Request:**
- Review Phase 1 Deliverables document for logical flow and completeness
- Create formal login/credentials document for teammate onboarding
- Assume teammate has no existing credentials

**Major Pivot - Architectural Decision:**
- Replace GHL (Go High Level) automation with N8N workflows for Phase 1 payment automation
- Use Stripe Checkout embedded in Next.js instead of GHL payment landing pages
- Rationale: Client stays in Pipedrive, N8N already needed for Phase 2, simpler tech stack

**Secondary Requests:**
- Verify Valta website GitHub repo and Vercel project names
- Check last deployment date
- Mark obsolete staging project (`valta-frontend-staging-2025`) as not to be used
- Add N8N Cloud credentials to credentials document
- Identify which API credentials are actually configured in N8N

## 2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **N8N Workflows**: Payment automation replacing GHL
  - Workflow 1: LOE signed → Create Stripe session → Send payment email
  - Workflow 2: Stripe webhook → Update Supabase/Pipedrive/ClickUp
- **Stripe Checkout**: Payment processing embedded in Next.js
- **Next.js Payment Page**: Custom page at `/payment/:jobId` or `/checkout/:sessionId`
- **Supabase Edge Functions**: Serverless functions for webhooks and integrations
- **Vercel Deployment**: GitHub to Vercel auto-deploy from main branch
- **Pipedrive CRM**: Primary CRM where client team works (not GHL)
- **ClickUp API**: Task auto-creation and updates
- **Resend API**: Email delivery (admin@valta.ca)
- **DocuSeal Webhooks**: Trigger N8N on LOE signature
- **Stripe Webhooks**: Trigger N8N on payment success

## 3. Files and Code Sections:

### **_work-in-progress/PHASE-1-DELIVERABLES.md** (16.4KB)
**Why Important**: Core testing checklist for Phase 1 launch
**Changes Made**: Replaced GHL payment approach with N8N + Stripe
```markdown
### 10. Payment Follow-Up Email (N8N Automation)

**N8N Workflow triggers on LOE signature:**
- [ ] DocuSeal webhook fires to N8N
- [ ] N8N queries Supabase for job details (amount, client email)
- [ ] N8N creates Stripe checkout session
- [ ] N8N generates payment email with checkout link
- [ ] Email sent via Resend (FROM: admin@valta.ca)
- [ ] Pipedrive deal updated: "Payment Requested"
- [ ] ClickUp task commented: "Payment email sent - $X,XXX"

### 11. Payment Page (Stripe Checkout / Next.js)

**Approach:** Stripe Checkout embedded in Next.js page on APR Dashboard

**Implementation:**
- Next.js page at `/payment/:jobId` or `/checkout/:sessionId`
- Stripe Checkout embedded (iframe or redirect)
- Valta branding wrapper around Stripe form
- SSL certificate on dashboard domain

### 12. Payment Confirmation & Job Activation (N8N Automation)

**N8N Workflow triggers on Stripe payment success:**
- [ ] Stripe webhook fires to N8N
- [ ] N8N updates Supabase: `payment_received = true`, `payment_date = now()`
- [ ] N8N sends confirmation email to client (receipt + next steps)
- [ ] Pipedrive deal updated: "Payment Received - Job Active"
```

### **_work-in-progress/LOGIN-CREDENTIALS-PHASE-1.md** (5.2KB)
**Why Important**: Single source of truth for Phase 1 credentials
**Changes Made**: Completely rewritten - removed GHL, added Stripe + N8N, simplified to just credentials
```markdown
## 7. Payment Processor (Stripe - Recommended)

**Purpose:** Process client payments via Next.js embedded checkout

**Login:**
- Dashboard: https://dashboard.stripe.com
- Username: [Provide]
- Password: [Provide]

**API Configuration:**
```
Publishable Key: [Provide - safe for frontend]
Secret Key: [Provide - server-side only, stored in Supabase secrets]
Webhook Secret: [Provide - for payment confirmation webhooks]
```

**Integration:**
- Next.js payment page: `/payment/:jobId` or `/checkout/:sessionId`
- Stripe Checkout embedded (branded to match valta.ca)
- Webhooks to N8N for payment confirmation automation

## 8. N8N Cloud (Payment Automation)

**Login:**
- URL: https://crowestudio.app.n8n.cloud
- Dashboard: https://crowestudio.app.n8n.cloud/home/workflows

**API Configuration:**
```
API Key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIwMTk0YTI1Yy0yNGJjLTRlYTMtYmFlYy05NGI2Nzc1OWMwYjkiLCJpc3MiOiJuOG4iLCJhdWQiOiJwdWJsaWMtYXBpIiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ5ODM3MDE5fQ.QwfGCt0qYQAcNnoQJ5QLU6s7YFzXOJeCHJpp3K5mJdk
Endpoint: https://crowestudio.app.n8n.cloud
```

**Workflows for Phase 1:**
1. Payment Email Workflow (LOE signed → email with Stripe link)
2. Payment Confirmation Workflow (Stripe webhook → update Supabase/Pipedrive/ClickUp)

**Credentials Stored in N8N:**
- Supabase API key
- Stripe Secret key
- Resend API key
- Pipedrive API key
- ClickUp API key
```

## 4. Errors and Fixes:

### **Error 1: Overcomplicated Credentials Document**
- **Problem**: Initial LOGIN-CREDENTIALS-PHASE-1.md was 20.3KB with extensive "how to login" instructions, testing steps, branding requirements
- **User Feedback**: 
  - "I don't want to see any payment landing page must have Valta branding. I don't want any of that"
  - "It should just be an API and Logging credentials page"
  - "I don't think you need to have how to log in to websites"
- **Fix**: Completely rewrote document, reduced to 5.5KB with only login URLs, usernames, passwords, API keys

### **Error 2: Incorrect Deployment Date**
- **Problem**: User expected last deployment around Oct 29 (Friday)
- **Investigation**: Git logs showed last commit Oct 23
- **Resolution**: Discovered Oct 29 activity was in APR Dashboard project, not Valta Website. Valta Website last deployed Oct 23.
- **User Response**: Accepted after verification

### **Error 3: Edit String Mismatch**
- **Problem**: `mcp__acp__Edit` failed because exact string didn't match (escape characters)
- **Fix**: Used `fast_read_file` to get exact line content, then corrected edit string

## 5. Problem Solving:

**Solved Problems:**

1. **Valta Staging Project Confusion**
   - **Problem**: `valta-frontend-staging-2025` listed as staging but not connected to any repo
   - **Solution**: Verified via `.vercel/project.json` that only `valta-live-repo` is connected
   - **Action**: Marked staging project as obsolete in all documentation

2. **GHL vs N8N Architecture Decision**
   - **Problem**: GHL adds complexity, client doesn't use it
   - **Solution**: Replace with N8N (already needed for Phase 2) + Stripe (industry standard)
   - **Benefits**: Simpler stack, client stays in Pipedrive, fewer platforms to manage

3. **Payment Page Implementation**
   - **Problem**: How to provide branded payment experience
   - **Solution**: Next.js page on dashboard domain with Stripe Checkout embedded, Valta branding wrapper
   - **Approach**: `/payment/:jobId` route with branded layout around Stripe form

4. **Credentials Discovery**
   - **Problem**: Which API keys are actually configured
   - **Solution**: Searched Supabase Edge Functions, .env.local, N8N credentials file
   - **Found**: ClickUp, Resend, DocuSeal, Supabase, N8N Cloud API keys
   - **Missing**: Stripe (client to provide), Pipedrive (client may have)

## 📝 Usage Instructions

### For Agents/Developers:
1. **Finding Credentials:** Search this file for the service name
2. **Adding New Credentials:** Add under appropriate category with date
3. **Updating Credentials:** Update the date and mark old ones as deprecated
4. **Never commit:** This file should be in .gitignore

### File Location Reference:
**Path:** `/Users/bencrowe/Development/01-Knowledge-Base/Logins & APIs/CRITICAL-API-KEYS.md`

### Memory System Integration:
- **DO NOT** ingest this file into Pinecone (contains secrets)
- **DO** add reference pointer in memory ("credentials are at /01-Knowledge-Base/Logins & APIs/")
- **DO** update this file whenever new APIs are added
- **DO** keep "Last Updated" date current at top

# 08-ClickUp-Integration - Project Management & Task Automation

**Last Updated**: September 30, 2025
**Status**: ✅ WORKING - Manual trigger via button

## 🎯 Purpose

Complete ClickUp integration for APR Dashboard: manual task creation → template with 9 subtasks → task tracking → ClickUp workspace synchronization.

## 📊 System Overview

### Complete Workflow
```
1. Admin reviews job in APR Dashboard
2. Clicks "Create ClickUp Task" button
3. Edge Function creates task in ClickUp
4. Task uses template (9 subtasks auto-added)
5. Task details saved to Supabase
6. Button updates to "View in ClickUp"
7. Admin can click to open task in ClickUp
```

## 🔑 Key Components

### 1. ClickUp Button Component
**Location**: `/src/components/dashboard/job-details/actions/ClickUpAction.tsx`
**Features**:
- Create task button (if no task exists)
- View in ClickUp button (if task exists)
- Purple color indicates task exists
- Opens ClickUp task in new tab

### 2. Task Creation Edge Function
**Location**: `/supabase/functions/create-clickup-task/index.ts`
**What**: Creates ClickUp task with template and 9 subtasks
**Process**:
1. Fetches job details from Supabase
2. Builds task name: `VAL###### - Property Name, Address`
3. Creates task with template ID
4. Sets priority: Normal (3)
5. Sets status: "To Do"
6. Saves task ID and URL to database

### 3. Database Storage
**Table**: `job_submissions`
**Fields**:
- `clickup_task_id`: Task ID from ClickUp
- `clickup_task_url`: Direct link to task

## 🔧 ClickUp Configuration

### API Settings
```
API Key: pk_63967834_W45TQXNS33YE9O1CA0K8RZDLGIM5B2FU
Template ID: t-86b3exqe8 (LOE New Template 2025.01.09)
List ID: 901402094744 (Valta workspace - production)
Test List: 901703694310 (Ben's workspace - testing)
```

### Task Template (9 Subtasks)
When a task is created, it automatically includes:
1. Create & Send LOE
2. Plan Job
3. Pull (TTSZ)
4. Tour Property
5. Sale and Lease Comps
6. Build Front End
7. Complete Valuation
8. Send to Client
9. Book Job

## 📋 Task Details

### Name Format
`VAL###### - Property Name, Address`

Example: `VAL251004 - 123 Main Street, Calgary AB`

### Description Includes
- Link to APR Hub job
- Client information
- Property details
- Intended use
- Notes from job submission

### Priority & Status
- **Priority**: Normal (3) - shows in main task list
- **Status**: "To Do" - ready for team to start

## 🧪 Testing

### Quick Test
```bash
node 08-ClickUp-Integration/03-Testing/test-scripts/test-apr-clickup-integration.js
```

### Manual Testing Checklist
- [ ] Open any job in APR Dashboard
- [ ] Click "Create ClickUp Task" button
- [ ] Verify task appears in ClickUp
- [ ] Verify 9 subtasks are present
- [ ] Button changes to "View in ClickUp"
- [ ] Click button opens task in ClickUp
- [ ] Task name matches format
- [ ] Task description includes job details

## 🚨 Common Issues & Fixes

### Issue 1: 401 Unauthorized
**Cause**: Invalid or expired API key
**Fix**: Verify API key in Edge Function environment variables

### Issue 2: Task Created Without Subtasks
**Cause**: Template ID not applied or template doesn't exist
**Fix**: Verify template ID `t-86b3exqe8` exists in ClickUp workspace

### Issue 3: Button Doesn't Update
**Cause**: Database not saving task ID/URL correctly
**Fix**: Check Edge Function response handling and database update logic

### Issue 4: Duplicate Tasks Created
**Cause**: System doesn't prevent duplicate task creation
**Fix**: Button is disabled when task already exists (check `clickup_task_id` field)

## 🔐 Credentials & API Keys

**ClickUp API** (in `/supabase/functions/create-clickup-task/index.ts`):
```
API Key: pk_63967834_W45TQXNS33YE9O1CA0K8RZDLGIM5B2FU
API Base URL: https://api.clickup.com/api/v2
```

**Supabase**:
```
Project: ngovnamnjmexdpjtcnky
URL: https://ngovnamnjmexdpjtcnky.supabase.co
```

## 📁 Folder Structure

```
08-ClickUp-Integration/
├── 01-Documentation/
│   └── README.md                    # Original working documentation
├── 02-Templates/
│   └── (ClickUp template configurations - stored in ClickUp)
├── 03-Testing/
│   └── test-scripts/
│       ├── test-apr-clickup-integration.js    # Main integration test
│       ├── create-test-task.js                # Test task creation
│       ├── get-clickup-templates.js           # Fetch template info
│       ├── get-template-details.js            # Template structure
│       └── test-clickup-api.js                # API connection test
└── README.md                        # This file
```

## 🎯 Related Code Locations

### Main Application Code
- `/src/components/dashboard/job-details/actions/ClickUpAction.tsx` - Button component
- `/supabase/functions/create-clickup-task/index.ts` - Task creation logic

### Database Tables
- `job_submissions` - Task ID and URL storage

## 📋 Future Enhancements

### Priority 1: Automatic Status Sync
- Webhook integration to sync task status back to APR Dashboard
- Update job status when ClickUp task is completed
- Real-time status updates without manual refresh

### Priority 2: Bidirectional Updates
- Update ClickUp task when job details change in APR Dashboard
- Sync property information, client details, notes
- Keep ClickUp task description current

### Priority 3: Subtask Completion Tracking
- Track which subtasks are completed
- Show progress bar in APR Dashboard
- Alert when certain subtasks complete (e.g., "LOE Sent")

### Priority 4: Team Assignment
- Assign tasks to specific team members based on job type
- Auto-assign based on workload or location
- Support for multiple assignees

## 🔗 Related Documentation

- **Valcre Integration**: `/03-Valcre-Integration.Dev/` - Job creation and data sync
- **LOE E-Signature**: `/07-LOE-ESignature/` - LOE creation (Subtask #1)
- **Client Intake**: `/06-Client-Intake-Form/` - Initial job data capture
- **File Management**: `/04-FileManagement/` - Document storage

## ✅ Success Criteria

**ClickUp Integration is working when**:
1. ✅ Admin can click "Create ClickUp Task" button
2. ✅ Task created in ClickUp with correct name format
3. ✅ Task includes 9 subtasks from template
4. ✅ Task details include job information
5. ✅ Task ID and URL saved to Supabase
6. ✅ Button updates to "View in ClickUp"
7. ✅ Clicking button opens task in ClickUp
8. ✅ No duplicate tasks created for same job
9. ✅ Task appears in correct ClickUp list
10. ✅ Team can see and work on task immediately

## 🎯 Important Notes

- **Manual Only**: Tasks are NOT created automatically (disabled Sept 9, 2025)
- **Template Required**: The template ID adds the 9 subtasks automatically
- **One Task Per Job**: System prevents duplicate tasks for same job
- **Production List**: Tasks go to Valta workspace list `901402094744`
- **Testing List**: Use Ben's workspace list `901703694310` for testing

---

**AGENTS: This system is WORKING. Don't rebuild it. If there are issues, check the Common Issues section and test scripts before assuming something is broken.**

# APR Calculator Module

## Current Status: ✅ FOUND AND ADDED FROM CES PROJECT!
**Last Updated**: September 9, 2025

The standalone calculator components have been extracted from the Report Generator CES project and added here!

## What's Now In This Folder

### From the CES Report Generator Project:
1. **ComputationalTestPage-Standalone.tsx** - Complete standalone calculator page
2. **Comp-Test-Pg-VO-v2.tsx** - GOOD calculator implementation with CollapsibleSection
3. **calculation/** folder - All calculation components:
   - CostApproach.tsx
   - FinancialSummary.tsx
   - IncomeApproach.tsx
   - SalesComparison.tsx
   - ValidationResults.tsx

**Note**: The full Report Generator app (which includes these calculators) is in folder 14-Contract-Generator/Report-Generator/

## Features

### Calculation Methods
1. **Income Approach**
   - NOI (Net Operating Income)
   - Cap rate application
   - Property value calculation

2. **Sales Comparison**
   - Comparable properties analysis
   - Price per square foot calculations
   - Weighted adjustments

3. **Cost Approach**
   - Land value
   - Building cost
   - Depreciation factors

4. **Comprehensive**
   - Weighted combination of all three methods
   - Adjustable weight percentages
   - Confidence scoring

## Technical Implementation

### Core Functions
```javascript
const calculateValues = async (method, liveData) => {
  // Income, Sales, Cost, or Comprehensive calculations
  // Auto-calculates based on input data
  // Returns calculated property values
}
```

### Key Features
- Live recalculation on input changes
- Comparable property management (up to 5)
- Automatic price per sq ft calculations
- Weighted comprehensive valuations
- Confidence scoring for each method

## Migration Plan

**Current**: Working in APR-Testing-Console
**Future**: Move to APR Hub main application

### Migration Steps
1. Extract calculator logic from Index.tsx
2. Create dedicated calculator component
3. Integrate with APR Hub dashboard
4. Connect to job workflow
5. Save calculations to Supabase

## Usage in Testing Console

Access at: `http://localhost:8081` (when console is running)
- Section 3B: Property Details & Comparables
- Auto-populates from job data
- Manual override available
- Instant recalculation button

## Files to Extract

When migrating:
1. `useStandaloneCalculations` hook
2. Calculation logic functions
3. Comparable management state
4. UI components for input/display

---
*Working calculator to be migrated from Testing Console to main APR Hub*

## Phase Roadmap

### Phase Definitions

Each phase represents a major milestone in the workflow automation journey.

---

### PHASE 1: Form Submission & Storage [COMPLETE]

**Goal:** Capture client submissions and store in database

**Features:**
- Client form embedded from Valta Website
- Iframe integration with test mode
- Data saves to Supabase
- Dashboard displays submissions
- Email notification to team

**Status:** [COMPLETE] (Oct 2025)

**Known Issues:**
- Email notifications delayed (N8N fix planned)

---

### PHASE 2: Dashboard Interface [COMPLETE]

**Goal:** Provide internal team interface for managing jobs

**Features:**
- Job list view with search/filter
- Job detail view with 5 sections
- Auto-save on all fields
- Section 1: Client & Property Info (read-only)
- Section 2: LOE Quote & Valuation Details (editable)
- Section 3: Building Information (editable)
- Section 4: Data Gathering (basic structure)
- Section 5: Document Upload (basic functionality)
- Per-job URL routing (`/dashboard/job/:jobId`)
- Bookmarkable URLs

**Status:** [COMPLETE] (Oct 2025)

**Enhancements Planned:**
- Section 4 expanded to smart document hub
- Section 3 auto-population via Houski

---

### PHASE 3: Valcre Integration [COMPLETE]

**Goal:** Sync job data to Valcre job management system

**Features:**
- "Create Valcre Job" button
- API integration with field mapping
- Four-table architecture
- Returns VAL number
- Button state updates after creation
- Direct link to Valcre job
- Currency formatting

**Status:** [COMPLETE] (Oct 2025)

**Critical Fix Applied:**
- Retainer field uses correct API name (`Retainer`)

---

### PHASE 4: LOE Generation [COMPLETE]

**Goal:** Generate professional Letter of Engagement

**Features:**
- "Preview & Send LOE" modal
- 4-page HTML LOE document
- Live preview before sending
- All job details populated dynamically
- Professional formatting
- Email recipient override (testing)

**Status:** [COMPLETE] (Oct 2025)

---

### PHASE 5: Email & E-Signature [IN PROGRESS] 90% COMPLETE

**Goal:** Send LOE to client with signing link

**Features:**
- DocuSeal integration (create signing link)
- Email via Resend API
- Professional email template
- FROM: Valta Appraisals <admin@valta.ca>
- Signing link in email body
- Edge Function deployed

**Status:** [IN PROGRESS] 90% Complete
- [x] API working


<file-read-info>Read lines 434-101. </file-read-info>

### PHASE 6: E-Signature Workflow [IN PROGRESS] 80% COMPLETE

### PHASE 7: ClickUp Integration [DEPLOYED] Awaiting Client Acceptance Testing

**Goal:** Automated task management in ClickUp

**Current Implementation:**
- Task auto-created during form submission processing
- Edge Function triggered in job creation flow
- Task name format: "PENDING - Property Name, City"
- Template automatically adds 9 subtasks
- Task ID and URL saved to database
- Dashboard button shows "View Task" (link to existing task)

**How It Works:**
1. Client submits form
2. Form data saves to Supabase
3. Edge Function automatically creates ClickUp task
4. Task created with PENDING name and template subtasks
5. Task ID/URL stored in database
6. User sees "View Task" button (task already exists)
7. When Valcre job created, task name updates to "VAL##### - Property Name"

**Status:** [DEPLOYED] Awaiting client acceptance testing
- [x] Auto-creation on form submission
- [x] Template with 9 subtasks
- [x] Database stores task references
- [x] View Task button functional
- [ ] Needs: Formatted description enhancement
- [ ] Needs: Auto-update on Valcre creation (PENDING → VAL#)
- [ ] Needs: Status updates via webhooks

**Implementation Plan:**
1. Enhance create-clickup-task Edge Function (formatted description)
2. Create update-clickup-task Edge Function
3. Add trigger in valcre.ts after job creation
4. Test end-to-end automation

**For detailed ClickUp implementation reference, see Appendix H.**

---

### PHASE 8: N8N Automations [PLANNED]

**Goal:** Advanced workflow automation for complex tasks

**5 Workflows Designed:**

#### 1. Missing Documents AI Review
- Trigger: Form submission
- AI reviews uploaded documents
- Flags missing critical documents
- Sends automated follow-up email
- Updates ClickUp with checklist

#### 2. Google Drive Folder Creation
- Trigger: Valcre job created (VAL# assigned)
- Creates folder from template
- Names: "VAL251019 - Property Name"
- Uploads client documents
- Organizes into subfolders
- Shares link with team

#### 3. Payment Follow-up (GHL Integration)
- Trigger: LOE signed
- GHL automation sends payment request email
- Branded landing page for payment collection
- Email sequence for follow-ups
- Updates job status to "payment_requested"

#### 4. Payment Tracking & Job Activation
- Trigger: GHL webhook (payment received)
- Updates job status to "payment_received"
- Assigns job to field appraiser in ClickUp
- Activates job in system
- Notifies team

#### 5. Houski Data Auto-Population with Validation
- Trigger: Address entered in Section 3
- Calls Houski API (14 fields)
- Cross-references with Google Maps
- Claude AI validates data consistency
- Populates fields if validated (>80% confidence)
- Flags for manual review if suspicious

**Status:** [PLANNED] Workflows designed, N8N setup pending

**Dependencies:**
- N8N Cloud account setup
- Google Drive API access
- Houski API credentials

---

### PHASE 9: Advanced Document Hub [PLANNED]

**Goal:** Intelligent document gathering and management (Section 4)

**Features:**
- Smart document categorization
- Auto-gathering from municipal websites
- PDF data extraction (AI agent)
- Visual progress tracking
- One-click export to Valcre
- Document validation

**Status:** [PLANNED] Designed, implementation pending

**See:** `_PHASE-2-DOCS/SECTION-4-DOCUMENT-HUB-PLAN.md`

---

### PHASE 10: Analytics & Reporting [FUTURE]

**Goal:** Business intelligence and performance tracking

### PHASE 11: Property Valuation Calculator [READY TO INTEGRATE]

**Goal:** Professional property valuation calculator integrated into APR Dashboard

**Business Value:**
- Provides appraisers with instant valuation estimates using three industry-standard methods
- Reduces manual calculation time from hours to minutes
- Ensures consistent methodology across all appraisals
- Generates confidence scores to validate final valuations
- Supports comprehensive analysis by weighting multiple approaches

**Location:** `/Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v2-Res/01-APR-Resources/08-Calculator/`

**Components Ready:**
1. `ComputationalTestPage-Standalone.tsx` (85KB) - Complete standalone calculator page
2. `Comp-Test-Pg-VO-v2.tsx` (73KB) - Calculator with CollapsibleSection UI
3. `calculation/` folder - Core calculation modules:
   - `CostApproach.tsx` - Land value + building cost - depreciation
   - `IncomeApproach.tsx` - NOI and cap rate calculations
   - `SalesComparison.tsx` - Comparable properties analysis (up to 5 comps)
   - `FinancialSummary.tsx` - Final valuation summary
   - `ValidationResults.tsx` - Confidence scoring and validation

**Calculation Methods:**

#### 1. Income Approach
- **Purpose:** Values property based on income-generating potential
- **Inputs:** Net Operating Income (NOI), Capitalization Rate
- **Calculation:** Property Value = NOI ÷ Cap Rate
- **Best For:** Rental properties, commercial real estate, multi-family buildings

#### 2. Sales Comparison Approach
- **Purpose:** Values property based on recent sales of similar properties
- **Inputs:** Up to 5 comparable properties with adjustments
- **Calculation:** Price per sq ft × Subject property sq ft ± adjustments
- **Features:**
  - Automatic price per square foot calculations
  - Location, condition, and amenity adjustments
  - Weighted averaging of multiple comps
- **Best For:** Residential properties, standardized commercial spaces

#### 3. Cost Approach
- **Purpose:** Values property based on replacement cost
- **Inputs:** Land value, building cost, depreciation factors
- **Calculation:** Land Value + (Building Cost - Depreciation)
- **Best For:** New construction, unique properties, insurance valuations

#### 4. Comprehensive Valuation
- **Purpose:** Combines all three methods with customizable weights
- **Features:**
  - Adjustable percentage weights for each method
  - Automatic weighted average calculation
  - Confidence scoring based on data quality
  - Final recommended value with supporting data
- **Best For:** Complex properties requiring multiple perspectives

**Key Features:**
- **Live Recalculation:** All values update instantly as inputs change
- **Comparable Management:** Add, edit, and remove up to 5 comparable properties
- **Auto-Save Integration:** Can connect to job workflow for persistent storage
- **Validation & Confidence Scoring:** AI-powered validation of input consistency
- **Professional Output:** Export-ready calculations for client reports

**Integration Plan:**

**Step 1: Add to Dashboard Navigation**
- Create new tab/section in job detail view
- Position after "Building Information" (Section 3)
- Label: "Valuation Calculator" or "Property Valuation"

**Step 2: Connect to Job Data**
- Auto-populate property details from Section 1 (address, sq ft, type)
- Pull building information from Section 3
- Link to Houski data when available (Phase 8)

**Step 3: Supabase Storage**
- Create `property_valuations` table
- Store calculation inputs and results per job
- Enable historical tracking of valuation changes
- Schema:
  ```sql
  - job_id (FK to job_submissions)
  - income_approach_value
  - sales_comparison_value
  - cost_approach_value
  - comprehensive_value
  - method_weights (JSON)
  - comparable_properties (JSON array)
  - confidence_score
  - created_at
  - updated_at
  ```

**Step 4: Export to Reports**
- Generate PDF summary of calculations
- Include in final appraisal report
- Export to Valcre when job created

**Technical Notes:**
- Extracted from CES Report Generator project
- Currently working in APR-Testing-Console
- React/TypeScript components ready to migrate
- Uses `useStandaloneCalculations` hook for state management
- No external dependencies beyond existing tech stack

**Status:** [READY TO INTEGRATE] Components built and tested, awaiting Phase 2+ migration

**Migration Checklist:**
- [ ] Copy calculator components to APR-Dashboard-v3
- [ ] Create Supabase table for valuation storage
- [ ] Add calculator route to dashboard
- [ ] Connect auto-population from job data
- [ ] Implement save/load functionality
- [ ] Add export to PDF feature
- [ ] Test with real job data
- [ ] Deploy to production

**Future Enhancements:**
- AI-powered comparable property suggestions
- Automatic data gathering from MLS listings
- Historical trend analysis for cap rates
- Market condition adjustments
- Multi-property batch calculations

---
```

**Tool Call: Read /Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v3/supabase/functions/send-loe-email-fixed/index.ts**
Status: Completed

```
import { serve } from 'https://deno.land/std@0.220.0/http/server.ts';

serve(async (req) => {
  const corsHeaders = {
    'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
    'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type',
  }

  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return new Response('ok', { headers: corsHeaders })
  }

  try {
    const { to, clientName, signingLink, propertyAddress } = await req.json()
    
    if (!to || !signingLink) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: 'Missing required fields: to and signingLink' }),
        { 
          status: 400,
          headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }
        }
      )
    }

    console.log('Sending LOE email to:', to)
    
    // Use Resend API - clean HTML emails without encoding issues
    const RESEND_API_KEY = 're_8B4Po2eL_84kFfQeEHAf4z4GFTLaqTv94';
    
    const emailHtml = `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
</head>
<body style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #333;">
<div style="max-width: 600px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 20px;">
<p>Hi ${clientName || 'Valued Client'},</p>
<p>Your Letter of Engagement for the property appraisal at ${propertyAddress || 'your property'} is ready for your review and signature.</p>
<p><a href="${signingLink}" style="background-color: #1a73e8; color: white; padding: 10px 20px; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 5px; display: inline-block;">Review & Sign Document</a></p>
<p>What happens next:</p>
<ul>
<li>Review the Letter of Engagement carefully</li>
<li>Sign the document electronically</li>
<li>You'll receive a copy for your records</li>
<li>We'll begin work on your appraisal immediately</li>
</ul>
<p>If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to contact us.</p>
<p>Best regards,</p>
<p><strong>Client Services Team</strong><br>
Valta Property Valuations Ltd.<br>
#300-4838 Richard Road SW, Calgary, AB T3E 6L1<br>
office: 587-801-5151 | email: clientcare@valta.ca | web: www.valta.ca</p>
</div>
</body>
</html>`;

    // Send via Resend
    const response = await fetch('https://api.resend.com/emails', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${RESEND_API_KEY}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        from: 'Valta Appraisals <noreply@crowestudio.com>',
        to: [to],
        subject: 'Letter of Engagement - Ready for Signature',
        html: emailHtml,
      }),
    });

    if (!response.ok) {
      const error = await response.text();
      throw new Error(`Resend API error: ${error}`);
    }

    const data = await response.json();
    console.log('Email sent successfully via Resend:', data.id);
    
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ 
        success: true, 
        message: 'Email sent successfully',
        recipient: to,
        emailId: data.id
      }),
      { 
        status: 200,
        headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }
      }
    )
    
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error in send-loe-email function:', error)
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ 
        error: 'Failed to send email',
        details: error.message 
      }),
      { 
        status: 500,
        headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }
      }
    )
  }
})
```

# Client Intake Form Module

## Current Status: ✅ WORKING - Built into APR Hub

**PURPOSE**: CLIENT-FACING FORM for appraisal requests. This is what CLIENTS fill out on your website to request an appraisal. They do NOT set prices - they just tell you what they need appraised.

## What This Form Is
- **Client-facing** submission form
- Designed to be embedded on public website
- Clients submit appraisal requests
- NO pricing fields (clients don't set their own fees!)
- Feeds into APR Hub workflow

## Location in Codebase

### Main Components
```
/src/components/
├── SubmissionForm.tsx              # Main form component
└── submission-form/                # Form sections
    ├── ClientInformationSection.tsx
    ├── ClientInformationSectionWithAutoFill.tsx
    ├── PropertyInformationSection.tsx
    ├── DocumentsSection.tsx
    ├── SubmitButton.tsx
    ├── SuccessScreen.tsx
    ├── useFormSubmission.ts        # Form logic hook
    └── index.ts
```

### Standalone Version
```
/src/pages/StandaloneForm.tsx      # Standalone page for testing
```

## Features

### Address Autocomplete (Google Places)
- **Status**: Setup guide available, not yet implemented
- **File**: `GOOGLE-PLACES-SETUP.md` (moved from _MODULES/08-Address-Autocomplete)
- **Purpose**: Auto-suggest validated addresses as client types
- **Like**: Pipedrive CRM address feature
- **Needs**: Google Places API key

### Client Information Section (CLIENT FILLS)
- First name, last name
- Email, phone
- Organization/company
- Address fields
- Auto-fill from client profiles

### Property Information Section (CLIENT FILLS)
- Property address
- Property name
- Property type dropdown
- Intended use dropdown
- Asset condition
- Special notes/instructions

### Financial Details (ADDED BY TEAM LATER)
- Appraisal fee - Set by appraiser after review
- Payment terms - Determined by company policy
- Retainer amount - Calculated based on fee
- These are NOT in client form - added during LOE preparation

### Document Upload
- File attachments
- Multiple document support
- Document type selection

## Form Workflow

1. **CLIENT fills form** → Their info + property they need appraised
2. **Form validation** → Required fields check
3. **Submit to Supabase** → Creates job request record
4. **TEAM reviews** → Determines fee, payment terms, etc.
5. **TEAM adds pricing** → When creating LOE
6. **Trigger webhooks** → Valcre, ClickUp, DocuSeal
7. **Success screen** → Client sees confirmation

## Integration Points

### Supabase
- Saves to `detailed_jobs` table
- Generates unique job ID
- Triggers Edge Functions

### Auto-Fill System
- Pulls from `client_profiles` table
- Matches by email or phone
- Pre-populates known client data

### Webhook Triggers
On successful submission:
1. Creates Valcre job → Returns CAL number
2. Creates ClickUp task → Workflow tracking
3. Prepares LOE → Ready for e-signature

## Access Points

### Current Testing URLs  
- **CORRECT FORM WITH TEST BUTTON**: http://localhost:8080/appraisal-request-form
- **Component**: `/src/components/SubmissionForm.tsx` (the real one with TestDataButton)
- **Route**: In App.tsx as `<Route path="/appraisal-request-form" element={<Index />} />`
- **Test Button**: Yes! Has "Fill Test Data" button that auto-fills form

### Alternative Form (different, simpler version)
- **URL**: http://localhost:8080/form  
- **Component**: `/src/pages/StandaloneForm.tsx`
- **Note**: Different form, no test button, may be for different purpose

### Main App Integration
- Embedded in dashboard
- Part of job creation flow

### Future Deployment
- **Plan**: Deploy to Valta website for direct client access
- **Future URL**: https://valta.ca/appraisal-request (or similar)
- **Purpose**: Allow clients to submit appraisal requests directly
- **Status**: Currently in APR Hub, ready to deploy as standalone

## Test Data
- Test data button available
- Auto-fills with sample data
- Useful for development

## Key Files

### Form Logic
- `/src/components/submission-form/useFormSubmission.ts`
  - Form state management
  - Validation logic
  - Submission handling
  - Webhook coordination

### API Endpoints
- `/api/jobs/create` - Job creation
- `/api/clients/autofill` - Client lookup

## Notes

- Form is responsive and mobile-friendly
- Built with shadcn/ui components
- TypeScript for type safety
- Integrated error handling

---
**MODULE STATUS**: Fully built and working - no additional development needed

# Google Places Address Autocomplete Setup

## Overview
Just like Pipedrive CRM, we can use Google Places API to provide smart address autocomplete that shows real, validated addresses as users type.

## What It Does
- User types: "123 main"
- Suggestions appear instantly:
  - "123 Main Street, Calgary, AB T2P 1A1, Canada"
  - "123 Main Street NW, Edmonton, AB T5J 2Z2, Canada"
  - "123 Main Avenue, Vancouver, BC V6B 5J3, Canada"
- User clicks one → Address is perfectly formatted and validated

## Setup Steps

### 1. Get Google Places API Key

1. Go to [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com)
2. Create a new project or select existing
3. Enable **Places API** and **Maps JavaScript API**
4. Go to "Credentials" → Create API Key
5. Restrict the API key:
   - Application restrictions: HTTP referrers
   - Add your domains: `localhost:*`, `your-domain.com`
   - API restrictions: Places API, Maps JavaScript API

### 2. Add to Your App

Add this to your `index.html` before closing `</body>`:
```html
<script async defer 
  src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=YOUR_API_KEY&libraries=places">
</script>
```

### 3. Environment Variable

Add to `.env`:
```
VITE_GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
```

### 4. Usage in Forms

Property Address field with autocomplete:
```tsx
import { AddressAutocomplete } from "@/components/ui/AddressAutocomplete";

<AddressAutocomplete
  name="propertyAddress"
  value={formData.propertyAddress}
  onChange={(value) => handleChange({ target: { name: 'propertyAddress', value }})}
  placeholder="Start typing property address..."
  country="CA" // Restricts to Canada
/>
```

## Features You Get

### 1. **Smart Predictions**
- Shows addresses as you type (after 3 characters)
- Prioritizes nearby locations
- Shows full formatted address

### 2. **Address Components**
- Street number and name
- City, Province, Postal Code
- Country
- All properly formatted

### 3. **Validation**
- Only real addresses appear
- No typos or invalid addresses
- Consistent formatting

### 4. **International Support**
- Works globally
- Can restrict to specific countries
- Multi-language support

## Cost

Google Places API pricing (as of 2024):
- **Autocomplete (Per Session)**: $2.83 per 1,000 sessions
- **Place Details**: $17 per 1,000 requests (if you need extra details)
- **Monthly Free Credit**: $200 (covers ~70,000 autocomplete sessions)

For an appraisal business:
- 100 forms/day = 3,000/month
- Cost: ~$8.50/month (well within free tier)

## Alternative: Mapbox

If you prefer Mapbox (also great):
```bash
npm install @mapbox/mapbox-gl-geocoder
```

Benefits:
- Similar autocomplete features
- Often cheaper for high volume
- Better map customization
- Good Canadian coverage

## Implementation Status

### ✅ Created:
- Mock autocomplete component (works without API key)
- Google Places integration template
- Proper error handling

### 🔄 To Do:
1. Add your Google API key
2. Test with real addresses
3. Add to both property and client address fields
4. Consider caching frequent addresses

## Testing Without API Key

The current implementation includes mock addresses for testing:
- Works immediately without setup
- Shows the UI/UX flow
- Easy to replace with real API

## Pro Tips

1. **Session Tokens**: We use session tokens to group autocomplete requests (saves money)
2. **Debouncing**: Wait 300ms after user stops typing before searching
3. **Country Restriction**: Limit to Canada for faster, more relevant results
4. **Caching**: Consider caching popular addresses for your area

## Security

- **Never expose API key in code** - use environment variables
- **Restrict API key** to your domains only
- **Monitor usage** in Google Cloud Console
- **Set billing alerts** (though you'll likely stay in free tier)

This is exactly what Pipedrive and other professional CRMs use - it's the gold standard for address entry!
````

# APR Dashboard V3 - Systems Guide

**Version:** 3.1
**Last Updated:** November 2, 2025
**Status:** [IN PROGRESS] - Comprehensive Systems Documentation

---

## Document Purpose

This Systems Guide serves as the **single source of truth** for the complete APR Dashboard V3 vision, architecture, and implementation roadmap. It captures:

- Complete system architecture
- Current implementation status (what works today)
- Full feature roadmap (phases 1-7+)
- Technical configurations (ClickUp, Houski, N8N, APIs)
- Implementation timeline and priorities
- Missing information and blockers
- **Complete field mappings** (Dashboard → Database → Valcre API)
- **Complete database schema** (SQL definitions)
- **Known issues with prioritization**

**Use this document to:**
- Understand the complete project vision
- Know what's built vs what's planned
- Find all technical configuration details
- Plan implementation work
- Onboard new team members or AI agents
- Reference field mappings for API integration
- Execute database migrations

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Executive Summary](#executive-summary)
2. [Project Vision & Goals](#project-vision--goals)
3. [System Architecture](#system-architecture)
4. [Current Implementation Status](#current-implementation-status)
5. [Phase Roadmap](#phase-roadmap)
6. [Technical Configurations](#technical-configurations)
7. [N8N Automation Workflows](#n8n-automation-workflows)
8. [Implementation Timeline](#implementation-timeline)
9. [Known Issues & Blockers](#known-issues--blockers)
10. [Appendices](#appendices)

---

## Executive Summary

### What Is APR Dashboard V3?

**APR Dashboard** is a modern appraisal job submission and management platform for **Valta Property Valuations**. It streamlines the complete appraisal workflow from initial client submission through job completion.

**Core Problem Solved:**
Replaces fragmented manual processes (forms, emails, spreadsheets, multiple systems) with a unified web platform that automates data flow between client intake, internal workflow, external systems (Valcre, ClickUp, Pipedrive, GHL), and team coordination.

### Key Statistics

- **Production URL:** https://apr-dashboard-v3.vercel.app
- **Current Phase:** Phase 5 (Email LOE) - 70% complete
- **Tech Stack:** Next.js + React + TypeScript + Supabase + Vercel
- **Active Integrations:** Valcre API, ClickUp, DocuSeal, Resend Email, Pipedrive CRM, GHL Automation
- **Planned Integrations:** N8N, Google Drive, Houski

### System Components

```
CLIENT FORM → DASHBOARD → VALCRE → LOE/E-SIGNATURE → INVOICE → JOB ACTIVATION
    ↓            ↓          ↓            ↓                ↓            ↓
Supabase    5 Sections  Job Mgmt   DocuSeal        GHL Payment   Appraiser
                                                    Pipedrive
```

### Current Status (November 2025)

**✅ Working (Production):**
- Client form submission (iframe from Valta Website)
- Dashboard job list and detail views
- Auto-save across all fields
- Valcre job creation API
- LOE generation with live preview
- Email sending (DocuSeal signing links)
- Per-job URL routing (`/dashboard/job/:jobId`)
- ClickUp task auto-creation on form submission

**⏳ Partially Working:**
- E-signature flow (deployed, awaiting client acceptance testing)
- Email domain (valta.ca working, crowestudio.com optional)

**📋 Planned (Not Yet Built):**
- N8N automation workflows (5 workflows designed)
- Houski property data integration
- Google Drive folder creation
- Payment tracking
- Advanced document hub (Section 4)

---

## Project Vision & Goals

### Vision Statement

Transform APR Dashboard into the **complete appraisal workflow hub** where the Valta team manages everything from initial client submission through job completion, eliminating manual data entry, reducing errors, and enabling the team to focus on high-value appraisal work.

### Business Goals

1. **Reduce Manual Work:** Eliminate repetitive data entry tasks across systems
2. **Eliminate Errors:** Auto-validation prevents incorrect data from entering Valcre
3. **Improve Client Experience:** Faster turnaround, professional e-signatures, automated updates
4. **Enable Scaling:** Handle increased workload without additional admin overhead
5. **Centralize Workflow:** Single platform integrates all necessary tools

### User Goals

**For Chris (Lead Appraiser):**
- Quick job review and approval
- One-click Valcre job creation
- Professional LOE sending
- Clear status tracking

**For Yumi (Assistant):**
- Clear task assignments
- Easy document gathering
- Automated follow-ups
- Organized file management

**For Clients:**
- Simple submission process
- Professional e-signature experience
- Automated status updates
- Easy document upload

---

## System Architecture

### High-Level Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        CLIENT-FACING                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ • Valta.ca Website (Form)                                       │
│ • DocuSeal E-Signature Interface                                │
│ • Email Communications (via Resend)                             │
│ • GHL Payment Landing Pages (white-labeled)                     │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┘
                      │
                      ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   APR DASHBOARD V3 (Frontend)                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ • Next.js + React + TypeScript                                  │
│ • Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui                                      │
│ • Deployed: Vercel                                              │
│ • URL: https://apr-dashboard-v3.vercel.app                      │
│                                                                 │
│ Pages:                                                          │
│ • /                      → Test form (internal)                 │
│ • /dashboard             → Job list                             │
│ • /dashboard/job/[id]    → Job detail (5 sections)              │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┘
                      │
                      ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SUPABASE (Backend)                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ PostgreSQL Database:                                            │
│ • job_submissions                                               │
│ • job_loe_details                                               │
│ • job_property_info                                             │
│ • job_files                                                     │
│                                                                 │
│ Edge Functions:                                                 │
│ • send-loe-email-fixed                                          │
│ • docuseal-webhook                                              │
│ • create-clickup-task                                           │
│ • update-clickup-task (PLANNED)                                 │
│                                                                 │
│ Storage:                                                        │
│ • Client file uploads                                           │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┘
                      │
                      ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  EXTERNAL INTEGRATIONS                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ • Valcre API       → Job management system                      │
│ • ClickUp API      → Task management                            │
│ • DocuSeal         → E-signature                                │
│ • Resend API       → Email delivery                             │
│ • Pipedrive CRM    → Deal ticket management (B2B focused)       │
│ • GHL Automation   → Payment follow-up, email sequences         │
│ • N8N Cloud        → Workflow automation (PLANNED)              │
│ • Google Drive     → File storage (PLANNED)                     │
│ • Houski API       → Property data (PLANNED)                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Data Flow: Form Submission to Job Activation

```
CLIENT FORM SUBMISSION
         │
         ▼
    Supabase DB
    (job_submissions)
         │
         ├──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────┐
         │                          │                      │
         ▼                          ▼                      ▼
   DASHBOARD VIEW            N8N: Missing Docs    CREATE CLICKUP TASK
   Section 1 populated       Check (PLANNED)      (Auto-created)
         │                          │                      │
         │                          ▼                      ▼
         │                     Email follow-up      Task: "PENDING"
         ▼                          │                      │
   Chris fills Section 2            │                      │
   (Fee, Retainer, etc)             │                      │
         │                          │                      │
         ▼                          │                      │
   CREATE VALCRE JOB ◄──────────────┘                      │
         │                                                 │
         ├─────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
         │                 │
         ▼                 ▼
   Returns VAL#      N8N: Create Drive Folder (PLANNED)
         │                 │
         ▼                 ▼
   Update job_loe_details  Organize files
         │
         ├──────────────────────┐
         │                      │
         ▼                      ▼
   GENERATE LOE          UPDATE CLICKUP TASK
   (HTML preview)        (Name: "VAL##### - Property")
         │
         ▼
   SEND LOE EMAIL
   (DocuSeal link)
         │
         ▼
   CLIENT SIGNS
         │
         ▼
   DocuSeal Webhook
         │
         ├───────────────────────┬──────────────────────┐
         │                       │                      │
         ▼                       ▼                      ▼
   Update status:       N8N: Generate Invoice   UPDATE CLICKUP TASK
   "loe_signed"         (GHL Landing Page)      (Status: "LOE Signed")
         │                       │
         │                       ▼
         │              Email payment link to client
         │              (GHL automated sequence)
         │                       │
         │                       ▼
         │              GHL Webhook: Payment received
         │                       │
         ▼                       ▼
   UPDATE CLICKUP          Update status:
   Status: "Invoiced"      "payment_received"
                                 │
                                 ▼
                           ACTIVATE JOB
                           Assign to appraiser
```

### Database Schema (Four-Table Architecture)

**Why Four Tables?**
Separates concerns for cleaner queries, better performance, and future flexibility:
1. `job_submissions` - Initial client data (Section 1)
2. `job_loe_details` - Quote/LOE details (Section 2)
3. `job_property_info` - Building/property research (Section 3)
4. `job_files` - Document uploads (Section 4)

**See Appendix F for complete SQL schema.**

### Tech Stack Details

**Frontend:**
- React 18 + TypeScript
- Next.js (App Router)
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui components
- Vite build system

**Backend:**
- Supabase (PostgreSQL database)
- Supabase Edge Functions (Deno)
- Supabase Storage (file uploads)

**Integrations:**
- Valcre API (job management)
- ClickUp API (task management)
- DocuSeal (e-signature)
- Resend API (email delivery)
- Pipedrive CRM (deal ticket management)
- GHL Automation (payment follow-up, email sequences)
- N8N Cloud (workflow automation - planned)
- Google Drive API (file storage - planned)
- Houski API (property data - planned)

**Deployment:**
- Vercel (auto-deploy from `main` branch)
- Custom domain: apr-dashboard-v3.vercel.app

---

```
✅ Task auto-created on form submission
✅ Uses template with 9 subtasks
✅ Saves clickup_task_id and clickup_task_url to database
✅ Button displays as "View Task" link (task already exists)
✅ Edge Function creates task during job creation

```

**Note:** The "Create ClickUp Task" button in the UI is actually a "View Task" link - the task is already created automatically when the form is submitted. The button provides quick access to view the existing task in ClickUp.

## Phase Roadmap

### Phase Definitions

Each phase represents a major milestone in the workflow automation journey.

---

### PHASE 1: Form Submission & Storage [COMPLETE]

**Goal:** Capture client submissions and store in database

**Features:**
- Client form embedded from Valta Website
- Iframe integration with test mode
- Data saves to Supabase
- Dashboard displays submissions
- Email notification to team

**Status:** [COMPLETE] (Oct 2025)

**Known Issues:**
- Email notifications delayed (N8N fix planned)

---

### PHASE 2: Dashboard Interface [COMPLETE]

**Goal:** Provide internal team interface for managing jobs

**Features:**
- Job list view with search/filter
- Job detail view with 5 sections
- Auto-save on all fields
- Section 1: Client & Property Info (read-only)
- Section 2: LOE Quote & Valuation Details (editable)

### PHASE 11: Property Valuation Calculator [READY TO INTEGRATE]

**Goal:** Professional property valuation calculator integrated into APR Dashboard

**Business Value:**
- Provides appraisers with instant valuation estimates using three industry-standard methods
- Reduces manual calculation time from hours to minutes
- Ensures consistent methodology across all appraisals
- Generates confidence scores to validate final valuations
- Supports comprehensive analysis by weighting multiple approaches

**Location:** `/Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v2-Res/01-APR-Resources/08-Calculator/`

**Components Ready:**
1. `ComputationalTestPage-Standalone.tsx` (85KB) - Complete standalone calculator page
2. `Comp-Test-Pg-VO-v2.tsx` (73KB) - Calculator with CollapsibleSection UI
3. `calculation/` folder - Core calculation modules:
   - `CostApproach.tsx` - Land value + building cost - depreciation
   - `IncomeApproach.tsx` - NOI and cap rate calculations
   - `SalesComparison.tsx` - Comparable properties analysis (up to 5 comps)
   - `FinancialSummary.tsx` - Final valuation summary
   - `ValidationResults.tsx` - Confidence scoring and validation

**Calculation Methods:**

#### 1. Income Approach
- **Purpose:** Values property based on income-generating potential
- **Inputs:** Net Operating Income (NOI), Capitalization Rate
- **Calculation:** Property Value = NOI ÷ Cap Rate
- **Best For:** Rental properties, commercial real estate, multi-family buildings

#### 2. Sales Comparison Approach
- **Purpose:** Values property based on recent sales of similar properties
- **Inputs:** Up to 5 comparable properties with adjustments
- **Calculation:** Price per sq ft × Subject property sq ft ± adjustments
- **Features:**
  - Automatic price per square foot calculations
  - Location, condition, and amenity adjustments
  - Weighted averaging of multiple comps
- **Best For:** Residential properties, standardized commercial spaces

#### 3. Cost Approach
- **Purpose:** Values property based on replacement cost
- **Inputs:** Land value, building cost, depreciation factors
- **Calculation:** Land Value + (Building Cost - Depreciation)
- **Best For:** New construction, unique properties, insurance valuations

#### 4. Comprehensive Valuation
- **Purpose:** Combines all three methods with customizable weights
- **Features:**
  - Adjustable percentage weights for each method
  - Automatic weighted average calculation
  - Confidence scoring based on data quality
  - Final recommended value with supporting data
- **Best For:** Complex properties requiring multiple perspectives

**Key Features:**
- **Live Recalculation:** All values update instantly as inputs change
- **Comparable Management:** Add, edit, and remove up to 5 comparable properties
- **Auto-Save Integration:** Can connect to job workflow for persistent storage
- **Validation & Confidence Scoring:** AI-powered validation of input consistency
- **Professional Output:** Export-ready calculations for client reports

**Integration Plan:**

**Step 1: Add to Dashboard Navigation**
- Create new tab/section in job detail view
- Position after "Building Information" (Section 3)
- Label: "Valuation Calculator" or "Property Valuation"

**Step 2: Connect to Job Data**
- Auto-populate property details from Section 1 (address, sq ft, type)
- Pull building information from Section 3
- Link to Houski data when available (Phase 8)

**Step 3: Supabase Storage**
- Create `property_valuations` table
- Store calculation inputs and results per job
- Enable historical tracking of valuation changes
- Schema:
  ```sql
  - job_id (FK to job_submissions)
  - income_approach_value
  - sales_comparison_value
  - cost_approach_value
  - comprehensive_value
  - method_weights (JSON)
  - comparable_properties (JSON array)
  - confidence_score
  - created_at
  - updated_at
  ```

**Step 4: Export to Reports**
- Generate PDF summary of calculations
- Include in final appraisal report
- Export to Valcre when job created

**Technical Notes:**
- Extracted from CES Report Generator project
- Currently working in APR-Testing-Console
- React/TypeScript components ready to migrate
- Uses `useStandaloneCalculations` hook for state management
- No external dependencies beyond existing tech stack

**Status:** [READY TO INTEGRATE] Components built and tested, awaiting Phase 2+ migration

**Migration Checklist:**
- [ ] Copy calculator components to APR-Dashboard-v3
- [ ] Create Supabase table for valuation storage
- [ ] Add calculator route to dashboard
- [ ] Connect auto-population from job data
- [ ] Implement save/load functionality
- [ ] Add export to PDF feature
- [ ] Test with real job data
- [ ] Deploy to production

**Future Enhancements:**
- AI-powered comparable property suggestions
- Automatic data gathering from MLS listings
- Historical trend analysis for cap rates
- Market condition adjustments
- Multi-property batch calculations

### PHASE 12: Google Places Address Autocomplete [READY TO IMPLEMENT]

**Goal:** Smart address autocomplete for client forms and APR dashboard

**Business Value:**
- Eliminates typos and invalid addresses
- Speeds up form completion by 50%
- Ensures consistent address formatting across all jobs
- Professional UX matching industry standards (Pipedrive, Salesforce)
- Reduces data entry errors and follow-up corrections

**Location:** `/Users/bencrowe/Development/APR-Dashboard-v2-Res/01-APR-Resources/10-Google Places-enhansed field ux/`

**How It Works:**

User types: `"123 main"`

Dropdown suggestions appear:
- 123 Main Street, Calgary, AB T2P 1A1, Canada
- 123 Main Street NW, Edmonton, AB T5J 2Z2, Canada  
- 123 Main Avenue, Vancouver, BC V6B 5J3, Canada

User clicks → Perfect formatted address auto-fills all fields

**Features:**

1. **Real-Time Suggestions**
   - Shows validated addresses after 3 characters
   - Prioritizes nearby locations
   - Full formatted address preview

2. **Auto-Population**
   - Street number and name
   - City, Province, Postal Code
   - Country
   - Consistent Canadian formatting

3. **Validation Built-In**
   - Only real addresses appear
   - No invalid or misspelled addresses
   - Google-verified location data

4. **Configurable Restrictions**
   - Limit to Canada for faster results
   - City-specific searches
   - Multi-language support

**Where to Use:**

1. **Client Intake Form** (public-facing)
   - Property address field
   - Client address field (if needed)

2. **APR Dashboard** (internal)
   - Section 1: Property Information
   - Section 3: Building Information
   - Any address input fields

3. **LOE Generation**
   - Property address validation
   - Client address confirmation

**Implementation:**

**Step 1: Get Google API Key**
- Enable Places API and Maps JavaScript API
- Restrict to your domains
- Add billing (stays in free tier)

**Step 2: Add to Environment**
```bash
VITE_GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
```

**Step 3: Load Google Script**
```html
<script async defer 
  src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=YOUR_API_KEY&libraries=places">
</script>
```

**Step 4: Implement Component**
```tsx
<AddressAutocomplete
  name="propertyAddress"
  value={formData.propertyAddress}
  onChange={(value) => handleChange({ target: { name: 'propertyAddress', value }})}
  placeholder="Start typing property address..."
  country="CA" // Restricts to Canada
/>
```

**Cost Analysis:**

Google Places API Pricing (2024):
- Autocomplete: $2.83 per 1,000 sessions
- Monthly free credit: $200
- Free tier covers: ~70,000 autocomplete sessions/month

**For typical appraisal business:**
- 100 forms/day = 3,000/month
- Estimated cost: ~$8.50/month
- **Stays within free tier**

**Technical Implementation:**

**Optimization Features:**
1. **Session Tokens** - Group requests to save costs
2. **Debouncing** - Wait 300ms after typing stops
3. **Country Restriction** - Limit to Canada for speed
4. **Caching** - Store frequent addresses

**Security:**
- API key in environment variables only
- Domain restrictions enabled
- Usage monitoring via Google Cloud Console
- Billing alerts configured

**Testing Without API Key:**
- Mock component available
- Shows UI/UX flow with sample data
- Easy to swap for real API

**Alternative: Mapbox**
If Google pricing becomes concern:
- Similar autocomplete features
- Often cheaper at high volume
- Better map customization
- Good Canadian coverage

**Status:** [READY TO IMPLEMENT] Setup guide complete, mock component built

**Implementation Checklist:**
- [ ] Create Google Cloud project
- [ ] Enable Places API + Maps JavaScript API
- [ ] Generate and restrict API key
- [ ] Add to .env file
- [ ] Load Google script in index.html
- [ ] Replace mock component with real API
- [ ] Test on client intake form
- [ ] Add to dashboard address fields
- [ ] Configure billing alerts
- [ ] Monitor usage for first month
- [ ] Document for team

**Files Ready:**
- `GOOGLE-PLACES-SETUP.md` - Complete setup guide
- `README.md` - Integration overview
- Mock component template included
